about.title:
Engineer.
Builder.
Founder.
paragraphs: I'm a Mechanical Engineering student at UT Austin with a Minor in Entrepreneurship, building hardware systems where tight tolerances and high stakes are the standard.

My internship work spans defense manufacturing engineering at Northrop Grumman, developing Python ML and Power Automate pipelines for F-35 metrology data and designing production aids to optimize manufacturing processes. In my extracirriculars, I have led structural design changes on liquid bi-propellant rockets at TREL and redesigning dynamics assemblies for Longhorn Racing Electric.

Outside of engineering, I served as a founding member and Vice President of Texas PALS, a student organization providing free, high-quality tutoring to underserved Austin high school students. Furthermore, I held the title of Vice President of External Programming for Beta Theta Pi, where I organized large-scale philanthropic events, community outreach programs, and educational initiatives.

Currently, I'm independently building a new coaxial contra-rotating VTOL drone with thrust-vector control and cruise missile-style deployable wings. In parallel with this work, I'm also contributing to Cascade, an agentic AI research platform for university life sciences labs, where I lead operations and go-to-market strategy development.

I'm passionate about building real products that solve complex problems and have an impact on the world. I'm interested in aerospace, robotics, process optimization, and I'm always looking for opportunities to connect with like-minded people.

bullets: Developed a Python multivariate regression model for SMR laser tracker & laser gauge metrology data to estimate F-35 fixture loading vectors, targeting elimination of an NC condition affecting ~33% of units (2–10 hrs unplanned rework each).
Automated QA workflows using Power Automate for Callboard Alerts, reducing response times by 21.1% within three weeks.
Contributed to architecture of a private LLM for F-35, B-2, E-2D, and restricted programs; defined prompt hierarchy, RAG strategies, and test protocols.
Built a Power BI manager-suite dashboard with write-back and advanced item search capabilities to fully automate a manual reporting process, saving ~312 hours annually.

Designed, assembled, and validated 3D-printed production aids with precision depth gauges to reduce tool validation time by 25%, providing digital readouts for F-35 STOVL tool alignment. Implemented new production processes and directed work-cycle time studies.

Conducted root cause analyses on BusinessObjects manufacturing data to identify sealant disbond conditions, revealing an annual rework cost of over $800K and 2,800 hours, leading to standardized preloading and sealant curing processes.

Analyzed CMM Romer Arm metrology data through the development of investigative Tableau dashboards to identify alignment trends across all F-35 variants, then implemented new procedures and planning changes that reduced unit rework by 10-20 hours.
Directed design of a 36" smokeless fire pit — solving airflow and heat distribution challenges with welded stainless steel construction for high-rate production.

Developed fully parametric SOLIDWORKS CAD models with bending and die design for flexible iteration, optimizing structures for manufacturability and waste reduction, considering 316L stainless steel stock sizes.

Sole design engineer for a new-generation 3DOF reaction control system test rig using cold gas nitrogen thrusters; developed mechanical constraint equations to determine required thrust, operating pressure, and mass flow.
Modified TVC gimbal for pitch and yaw with 60° operating cone. Determined a 2-passage rotary union within budget and physical constraints, converting it into a controller for 8 separate fluid lines.
Designed an innovative slotted rack electronics board prioritizing serviceability and ease of wiring. System validated as an inverted pendulum for attitude and roll stabilization.
Designed, fabricated, and assembled front and rear unsprung assemblies for two consecutive UT FSAE Electric cars using milled 7075 aluminum for optimal stiffness-to-weight ratio.

Developed an original magnetic flux analytical framework to redesign wheel speed sensor architecture with 3-phase hall effect sensor array, achieving 2.5–5x signal amplitude improvement over prior design; selected cost-effective catalog magnets and validated on vehicle.
Developed adjustable camber, toe, and Ackermann steering systems for precision suspension tuning. Specialized tripod interface with steel inserts to transmit motor torque through half shafts.
Conducted comprehensive FEA including advanced modeling of bearing interactions and load transfers, achieving a minimum factor of safety of 2 under fatigue loading conditions.

academicProjects.label: New Venture Mechanics — In Progress
academicProjects.name: Cascade
academicProjects.desc: Founding member of a class startup building an agentic AI-powered research workflow platform for university medical and life sciences researchers. Developing go-to-market strategy, key feature work, and operational infrastructure.

academicProjects.label: ME 366J — Senior Design
academicProjects.name: Rotary Friction Test Bed
academicProjects.desc: Designing a tribometer-style apparatus to measure frictional torques on annular surfaces. High detail customer needs and engineering specifications that require full mechanical design, pneumatic actuator integration, and tribology data acquisition for friction characterization controlling factors such as load, speed, materials, surface geometry, and temperature.

academicProjects.label: ME 338 — Machine Elements
academicProjects.name: RC Race Car
academicProjects.desc: Machine Elements engineering project to design and manufacture a high-performance RC car capable of completing a loop course in the shortest time possible. As the sole product designer, created a custom drivetrain, wooden cantilever suspension system, and chassis using SOLIDWORKS, followed by manufacturing the components with Laser Cutting and 3D printing.

Connect.
contact.sub: Interested in robotics, aerospace, optimization, and advanced manufacturing engineering. Always happy to connect with people doing interesting work.
